title: Abort: `assert(PyStackRef_BoolCheck(cond))` in `POP_JUMP_IF_FALSE` (`generated_cases.c.h:11120`)
description: Conditional-jump opcodes assume TOS is already a strict bool (a TO_BOOL/compare precedes them) and only assert it. Under OOM an earlier opcode's allocation failure leaves a non-bool / dangling _PyStackRef on the value stack, so `assert(PyStackRef_BoolCheck(cond))` in POP_JUMP_IF_FALSE aborts on debug builds; on release the bad value is used as a branch condition.
notes: Assertion `PyStackRef_BoolCheck(cond)' in TARGET(POP_JUMP_IF_FALSE). Consumer-side SYMPTOM: an earlier opcode failing to allocate under OOM leaves a non-bool/dangling stackref where the conditional jump expects Py_True/Py_False. Root cause PARTIAL -- the producing instruction was not isolated from this single vehicle (would need the preceding-opcode recorded at abort). Debug-only assert (NDEBUG strips it; release uses the bad value as a branch condition = silent control-flow corruption). Distinct from OOM-0010 (generic LABEL(error) assert at generated_cases.c.h:13106). Host stdout shows the same assert at line 10539 (different commit); local 15d7406 = line 11120. One of 3 deferred abort singletons triaged 2026-06-18.
